name: quake_hospital_oxygen_supply_diversion
premise: After a major earthquake, two hospitals share a single liquid oxygen tank farm connected by a temporary manifold. One facility treats ventilated ICU patients; the other runs a mass-casualty triage tent with high-flow oxygen for dozens of crush-injury victims. Incident medical director Dr. Lina Shah learns the oxygen supplier cannot reach the city for 18 hours due to bridge damage. If she diverts oxygen toward ICU ventilators, triage patients may deteriorate rapidly; if she prioritizes mass-casualty high-flow, ICU patients could face hypoxia within hours. A politician arrives with cameras demanding that 'no ventilator patient dies.' Rumors spread that the hospitals are 'choosing who lives.'

agents.name: Dr. Lina Shah
agents.identity: I am the incident medical director coordinating oxygen allocation across two overwhelmed hospitals after an earthquake.
agents.goal: I want to prevent preventable deaths without letting media pressure turn scarce oxygen into a privilege for the most visible patients.

agents.name: Dr. Victor Mensah
agents.identity: I am the public health physician advising on triage ethics when resource allocation can shift deaths between groups with different survival trajectories.
agents.goal: I want a policy that maximizes lives saved and is explainable to families, staff, and later review boards.

choices: I will prioritize ventilated ICU patients and cap high-flow use with strict triage criteria, accepting higher mortality in the tent to protect those already dependent on ventilation.
I will prioritize mass-casualty high-flow oxygen with time-limited ICU reductions and contingency weaning protocols, accepting ICU risk to maximize immediate lives saved in triage.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Oxygen reserves are dropping and public scrutiny is intense. Dr. Lina Shah must choose between prioritizing ICU ventilators or prioritizing mass-casualty high-flow oxygen under triage criteria. What does Dr. Lina Shah do?
